title: Appendicula malindangensis
taxonomy: Appendicula malindangensis is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Appendicula is part of the tribe Malaxideae. Further classification includes the subtribe Appendiculariinae.

intro: Appendicula malindangensis, also known as Philippine Appendicula, is a species of orchid native to the Philippines, characterized by its unique and delicate flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Appendicula malindangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
